that the world around them is getting
worse, not better.
and my response to all of their
complaints is the same. Uh, and I want
to share that with you guys this
morning. Uh, and that is to say that um,
if you don't like it, go fix it. It's up
to us to treat people with kindness,
with with respect, with dignity. It's up
to us to change the world around us in
our homes. It's up to us to change the
world around us in our workplaces. It's
up to us to change our communities. And
ultimately, if we do enough of that on
the micro level, we'll see enough of
that reflected on the macro level. So, I
just want to say I love you. God bless
you guys. Appreciate you guys tuning in
to these uh every single day. So,
without further delay, uh let me get
into the executive summary from today's
lead off morning note. If you would like

conclusions and to see if you need to

remain on the right side of market risk
or to get on the right side of market
risk to the extent you haven't been
following Kiss or Dr. Mo, you need to be
a client of 42 macro. So today's macro
question is why aren't bears getting
paid despite fresh evidence of sticky
inflation and an unraveling labor market
in the US economy? Our answer in word
paradigm C. Sorry, that's two words, but
you get the point. For our fundamental
research summary, the Trump
administration is likely guiding the US
economy to paradigm C, which features
all the fiscal and monetary large that
perpetuated the K-shaped economy of
paradigm A, plus trillions more in tax
cuts, substantial deregulation, and some
reshoring of industries deemed critical
for national defense. In short, paradigm
C implies the net impact of fiscal,
regulatory, and trade policy changes
represents a durable positive shock to
growth that Wall Street consensus is
currently underpositioned for. Our
fundamental research summary also reads,
"The path that asset markets may take to
realize paradigm sees upside risk is not
linear. The worst of the U-shaped
economy is likely still ahead of us, and
the slowdown in growth will flatter
consensus recession fears. Trade policy
uncertainty is likely to persist as well
and may even deteriorate. We view these
transitory outcomes as scarecrows to be
faded by every investor with an
investment horizon that extends fast
this fall because the administration's
policy signaling continues to imply low
probability of a return to bearish
paradigm B. Focus on the forest, not the
trees. Since April, we have anchored
upon paradigm our paradigm C theme to
instill confidence among thousands of
professional and retail investors around
the world, helping them block out the
bare porn noise and maximize upside
capture in this raging bull market.
The bullish response uh the bullish
market response to today's unequivocally
bearish data gives us confidence to
reiterate our theme which we still
anticipate is likely to persist through
at least November of 2026 when the US
midterm elections occur. As we wrote
yesterday, if we're going to be proven
wrong on our paradigm C theme or if it's
about to take a hazardous intermission,

ultimately fail. Uh and and and so the
the goal of Paradigm C to recall is for
the um the fiscal authority to try to
pull the levers it's likely it can pull
and some others that it's trying to pull
that it technically doesn't have any

to GDP ratios to stabilize the debt and
ultimately outgrow it. Um, and so it's
our view that they're going to continue
to frantically pull every lever and
twist every knob in their in their
purview to engineer this outcome. It's
also our view that they will ultimately
fail because when you study uh the
history of uh you know these major
Republican tax cuts um you know going
back to the two tax cuts under President
Reagan, the two under President Bush and
obviously the uh the um the tax cuts and
jobs act under um Trump's first
administration. What we find from our
analysis is that only one of those task
programs uh created a durable positive
shock to growth on a long-term basis.
Four of the five created a durable
positive shock to the budget deficit on
a long-term basis. And all five created
a durable positive shock to the debt to
GDP ratio on a long-term basis. And so
it's very like unlikely that we outgrow
our way out of a 6% debt to GDP uh
deficit to GDP ratio year after year
after year. And so ultimately we're
going to wind up with a situation with
even more debt uh and even bigger
deficits u not anytime soon but this is
the likelihood over a multi-year time
horizon which ultimately means that the
biggest risk to the Treasury bond market
which has always been at the epicenter
of our forturning invested doing a
forturning regime analysis the biggest
risk to the geopolitically driven supply
and demand imbalance in the Treasury
bond market is paradigm C's ultimate
failure and what that likely means from
a socialist perspective in terms of
further a further lurching into the
populism and and fiscal dominance and
ultimately that will require a shift to
paradigm D which is the whatever that
administration's you know will be their
their desire to try to print their way
out of it much like we saw in ancient

1500s in Europe much like what we saw in
